name: MuleSoft Anypoint Platform API
what_it_does: MuleSoft Anypoint Platform — enterprise integration and API management platform for connecting on-premise and cloud systems via a unified API-led connectivity model with Mule runtime.
use_cases: Agents orchestrating complex enterprise data flows across SAP, Salesforce, and legacy mainframe systems
API-led connectivity — agents accessing reusable business APIs built and managed on Anypoint Exchange
Real-time event streaming — agents subscribing to Anypoint MQ for enterprise event-driven architectures
Legacy system modernization — agents wrapping SOAP, EDI, and mainframe interfaces as REST APIs via Mule
Data transformation at scale — agents using DataWeave for complex transformations between enterprise data formats
not_for: Startups or small teams — MuleSoft is enterprise-priced and enterprise-complexity
Developer-friendly code-first automation — use Temporal or Inngest for programmatic orchestration
Simple REST API proxying — use Kong or Zuplo for lightweight API gateway needs
best_when: Your organization already uses MuleSoft and needs agents to integrate with the existing Anypoint Platform ecosystem, or you're in a large enterprise with complex on-premise/cloud integration needs.
avoid_when: You need quick, code-first automation without enterprise infrastructure — the complexity and cost aren't justified.

auth.notes: Connected App OAuth2 for programmatic access. Personal Access Tokens for development. Business Group and Environment-scoped permissions. Role-based access control.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Anypoint Platform trial available; Mule runtime open source (Community Edition)
pricing.notes: Enterprise pricing only — contact sales. Community Edition of Mule runtime is open source. Salesforce acquisition makes pricing bundled for Salesforce customers.

performance.rate_limits: API Manager policies configurable; Anypoint MQ throughput plan-dependent; Management API: standard limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Anypoint Platform API returns JSON errors with error codes. Mule flow errors require CloudHub log inspection. Error granularity varies by connector.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: API deployments are idempotent. Message processing idempotency depends on flow design. Anypoint MQ supports at-least-once delivery.
known_agent_gotchas: Anypoint Platform API, CloudHub API, and Runtime Manager API are separate — different base URLs and auth
Mule 3 and Mule 4 APIs have significant differences — agents must target the correct runtime version
Deployment is async — application deployment returns immediately, agents must poll for running status
Business Group hierarchy affects API visibility — wrong organization ID returns empty results silently
DataWeave transformations run on Mule, not as API calls — agents cannot execute DataWeave remotely

security_score_components.security_notes: FedRAMP authorized. SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA. Secrets Vault for credential management. Role-based access with Business Group isolation. Owned by Salesforce.
